column1: # Seed corpus for the local speculative n-gram model (core/src/ngram.rs).
#     https://github.com/thunlp/THUOCL  — IT, food, diming (place names) only
#   Conversational supplement  (MIT, same as this repository)
#   weight 1–10 (log-scaled from jieba frequency, or rank-based for THUOCL)
#   lines starting with '#' or blank lines are ignored
# Single-char entries for characters that appear in many everyday phrases but are
# underweighted in jieba's standalone-word frequency (jieba is trained on news text
# where these characters mostly appear mid-compound, not as standalone words).
# With the single-char-only unigram policy in train_from_corpus, these entries are
# the *sole* source for the unigram distribution of the syllables listed here, so
# they must dominate any competing character.  Weights are set to beat the highest-
# weight single-char jieba entry for the same syllable by a comfortable margin.
# Reference: Junda and Pinard (2006) character frequency study; cross-checked with
# Jun Da's Modern Chinese Character Frequency List (freely available).
